title: ceratostylis heleocharis
intro: Ceratostylis heleocharis, also known as the orchid of the swamp or the marsh orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid that thrives in tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to the presence of certain compounds in its leaves and roots. The health benefits of Ceratostylis heleocharis include its potential to treat fever, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy in folk medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used to create infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are consumed orally to harness its therapeutic properties.
